story.iWant: Recipe to parse DNG Camera Profile (.dcp) files and extract tone curve adjustments
story.soThat: I can convert Adobe camera profiles to other preset formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle) and use them across different editing software

tasks.task: Set Up DCP Package Structure (AC: All) - Create internal/formats/dcp/ directory with types.go, parse.go, tiff.go, profile.go, parse_test.go, and testdata/dcp/ for sample files
Add github.com/google/tiff Dependency (AC-1) - Add dependency to go.mod and verify imports
Implement TIFF Reading (AC-1) - Implement readTIFF() and extractCameraProfileTag() helpers for TIFF structure and tag 50740 extraction
Implement Camera Profile XML Parsing (AC-2, AC-3, AC-4) - Implement parseProfile() for XML unmarshaling, tone curve parsing, and color matrix parsing
Analyze Tone Curve Shape (AC-3) - Implement analyzeToneCurve() to extract exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows from piecewise linear curve points
Implement Parse() Function (AC-5) - Orchestrate TIFF reading → XML extraction → tone curve analysis → UniversalRecipe mapping
Error Handling (AC-6) - Validate TIFF magic bytes, wrap errors with context, handle corrupt files gracefully, clamp out-of-range values
Acquire Real DCP Sample Files (AC-7) - Download Adobe DCP samples (Canon, Nikon, custom profiles) and add to testdata/dcp/ with README documenting sources
Write Unit Tests (AC-7) - Test Parse() with valid DCP, missing tone curve, non-identity matrices, corrupt TIFF, missing tag 50740, malformed XML, tone curve analysis edge cases. Target ≥85% coverage
Documentation (AC-1 to AC-6) - Add package comments, function documentation, update docs/parameter-mapping.md with DCP tone curve mappings, add testdata README

criterion.title: Parse TIFF-based DCP Structure
criterion.item: ✅ Read TIFF file using github.com/google/tiff library
✅ Validate TIFF magic bytes (II for little-endian, MM for big-endian)
✅ Parse TIFF Image File Directory (IFD) structure
✅ Extract standard TIFF tags (ImageWidth, ImageLength, etc.)
✅ Handle both standalone .dcp files and embedded DCP in DNG (standalone priority)
✅ Report parsing errors with clear messages (corrupt TIFF, missing IFD, etc.)

criterion.title: Extract Camera Profile XML
criterion.item: ✅ Locate TIFF tag 50740 (CameraProfile tag)
✅ Extract XML data from tag 50740
✅ Validate XML structure (check for Adobe Camera Raw namespace)
✅ Parse ProfileName element (profile identity)
✅ Handle missing tag 50740 gracefully (return error: "not a valid DCP file")
✅ Support DCP v1.0-v1.6 format variations

criterion.title: Parse Tone Curve Adjustments
criterion.item: ✅ Parse <crs:ToneCurve> XML element (piecewise linear curve points)
✅ Extract tone curve points as (input, output) pairs (0-255 range)
✅ Analyze tone curve shape to extract parameters: Exposure (midpoint shift), Contrast (curve slope), Highlights (top-end curve shape), Shadows (bottom-end curve shape)
✅ Handle missing tone curve (use linear curve as fallback)
✅ Clamp extracted values to valid UniversalRecipe ranges

criterion.title: Parse Color Matrices (Identity Matrices)
criterion.item: ✅ Parse <crs:ColorMatrix1> and <crs:ColorMatrix2> (if present)
✅ Recognize identity matrices (diagonal 1.0, off-diagonal 0.0)
✅ Skip full camera calibration (ForwardMatrix, CalibrationIlluminant) for MVP
✅ Log warning if non-identity matrices detected ("color calibration not supported")
✅ Store matrix data in Metadata map for future use (optional)

criterion.title: Return UniversalRecipe Representation
criterion.item: ✅ Map parsed tone curve to UniversalRecipe fields: recipe.Exposure, recipe.Contrast, recipe.Highlights, recipe.Shadows
✅ Preserve profile metadata (name, description if present)
✅ Store unsupported DCP data in Metadata map (matrices, HSV tables)
✅ Return populated *universal.Recipe and nil error on success

criterion.title: Handle Parsing Errors
criterion.item: ✅ Validate TIFF magic bytes before parsing (fail fast if not TIFF)
✅ Report specific errors (line number or tag ID if possible)
✅ Handle corrupt TIFF files gracefully (no panics)
✅ Handle malformed XML in tag 50740 (return descriptive error)
✅ Handle out-of-range tone curve values (clamp to 0-255)

criterion.title: Unit Test Coverage
criterion.item: ✅ Unit tests for Parse() function with real Adobe DCP samples
✅ Test edge cases (missing tone curve, identity matrices, corrupt TIFF)
✅ Test with minimum 3 real-world DCP files from Adobe
✅ Test coverage ≥85% for dcp/parse.go, dcp/tiff.go, dcp/profile.go
✅ All tests pass in CI

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Parse DCP (DNG Camera Profile) files per Adobe DNG Specification 1.6, generate DCP files from UniversalRecipe color adjustments, support XML-based camera profile structure, validate compatibility with Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Architecture components: internal/formats/dcp/ package with parse.go (TIFF reading → XML extraction), tiff.go (low-level TIFF tag operations using github.com/google/tiff), profile.go (Adobe camera profile XML parsing/generation), types.go (color matrices, tone curves, HSV tables). Key design: split TIFF operations from XML profile logic, use identity matrices for non-calibration use cases.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: Detailed Design - DCP File Structure and Tone Curve Analysis
doc.snippet: DCP files are TIFF containers with Adobe Camera Profile XML embedded in tag 50740. Focus on tone curve adjustments (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows) mapped to UniversalRecipe. Identity color matrices for non-calibration use. Tone curve analysis: midpoint shift → Exposure, curve slope → Contrast, top-end (192-255) → Highlights, bottom-end (0-64) → Shadows.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Go Struct Mapping
doc.snippet: CameraProfile struct with ToneCurve, ColorMatrix1/2. ToneCurve contains ToneCurvePoint array with Input/Output (0-255 range). Custom XML unmarshaling for "input, output" string format. Matrix struct with 3x3 float64 values for color transformation.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: APIs and Interfaces - Parse and Generate Functions
doc.snippet: Parse(data []byte) (*universal.Recipe, error): Read TIFF → extract tag 50740 → parse XML → analyze tone curve → return UniversalRecipe. Key helpers: readTIFF(), extractCameraProfileTag(), parseProfile(), analyzeToneCurve(), profileToUniversal(). Error handling with fmt.Errorf wrapping.

artifact.reason: Reference pattern for Parse() function signature, error handling with fmt.Errorf wrapping, validation flow (validateFileStructure → extractParameters → validateParameters → buildRecipe)
Reference pattern for XML parsing with encoding/xml, ConversionError wrapping, validateXMLStructure → unmarshal → extractParameters → validateParameters → buildRecipe flow
Reference pattern for parameter extraction and validation, ConversionError usage, buildRecipe construction without builder pattern
Most recent XML parser implementation (Story 8-1), shows current XML parsing patterns, metadata handling, parameter mapping with clamping
Complete UniversalRecipe struct definition showing all available fields for tone curve mapping (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Blacks, Whites), Metadata map for unsupported DCP data
Shows format detection and Parse() integration pattern for extending converter with new DCP format (Story 9-5 will integrate here)
Format detection logic pattern for adding DCP recognition (TIFF magic bytes II/MM + .dcp extension check)

go.reason: NEW DEPENDENCY - Complete TIFF library for reading/writing custom TIFF tags (tag 50740 CameraProfile). Go stdlib image/tiff is decoder-only and doesn't support custom tags.
Parse Adobe Camera Profile XML structure embedded in TIFF tag 50740
JSON marshaling for test fixtures and verbose output
Error wrapping with fmt.Errorf and %w verb
TIFF data stream handling with bytes.NewReader
Math operations for tone curve analysis (interpolation, clamping)

constraints.constraint: Follow exact pattern of existing format packages (np3, xmp, lrtemplate, costyle): Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error) signature
Maintain hub-and-spoke architecture: DCP → UniversalRecipe only (no direct format-to-format conversion)
Performance target: <200ms DCP parsing (slower than other formats acceptable due to TIFF overhead)
Test coverage: ≥85% for dcp package (parse.go, tiff.go, profile.go)
Error handling: Use fmt.Errorf with %w verb for error wrapping, no panics, all errors via return value
Validation: Fail-fast approach - validate TIFF magic bytes before parsing, check tag 50740 exists, validate XML structure
Focus on tone curves only: Skip full camera calibration (ForwardMatrix, ColorMatrix, dual illuminant) for MVP
Use identity color matrices: Diagonal 1.0, off-diagonal 0.0 (no camera-specific calibration)
Clamp extracted values: Exposure (-2.0 to +2.0), Contrast/Highlights/Shadows (-1.0 to +1.0)
Document limitations: Update docs/parameter-mapping.md with DCP tone curve mappings and unsupported features (color matrices, HSV tables, dual illuminant)

interface.name: Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)
interface.description: Main DCP parser function - validates TIFF structure, extracts camera profile XML from tag 50740, analyzes tone curve, returns UniversalRecipe or error

interface.name: readTIFF(data []byte) (*tiff.TIFF, error)
interface.description: Reads TIFF structure using github.com/google/tiff, validates magic bytes (II/MM), returns parsed TIFF or error

interface.name: extractCameraProfileTag(tf *tiff.TIFF) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Extracts XML bytes from TIFF tag 50740 (CameraProfile tag), returns error if tag not found

interface.name: parseProfile(xmlData []byte) (*CameraProfile, error)
interface.description: Unmarshals Adobe Camera Profile XML into CameraProfile struct, validates namespace, parses tone curve points and color matrices

interface.name: analyzeToneCurve(points []ToneCurvePoint) (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows float64)
interface.signature: func analyzeToneCurve(points []ToneCurvePoint) (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows float64)
interface.description: Analyzes tone curve shape to extract UniversalRecipe parameters: midpoint shift → Exposure, slope → Contrast, top-end (192-255) → Highlights, bottom-end (0-64) → Shadows

interface.name: profileToUniversal(profile *CameraProfile) *models.UniversalRecipe
interface.description: Converts parsed DCP CameraProfile to UniversalRecipe, calls analyzeToneCurve(), stores profile name in metadata, logs warning if non-identity matrices detected

interface.name: CameraProfile struct
interface.signature: type CameraProfile struct { XMLName xml.Name; ProfileName string; ToneCurve *ToneCurve; ColorMatrix1/2 *Matrix }
interface.description: Represents Adobe DNG Camera Profile XML structure, includes tone curve points and color matrices, supports custom XML unmarshaling for "input, output" format

interface.name: ToneCurvePoint struct
interface.signature: type ToneCurvePoint struct { Input int; Output int } // 0-255 range
interface.description: Single point on piecewise linear tone curve, custom UnmarshalXML/MarshalXML for "input, output" string format parsing

tests.standards: Unit tests follow Recipe's established patterns: table-driven tests with real sample files in testdata/ directory, descriptive test names with Test{Function}_{Scenario} format, assertion of both success cases and error conditions, coverage measured with go test -cover targeting ≥85%. DCP tests use real Adobe sample files (Canon, Nikon, custom profiles) acquired from Adobe Camera Raw SDK or DNG Profile Editor. Test organization: parse_test.go (Parse function tests), tiff_test.go (TIFF tag operations), profile_test.go (tone curve analysis algorithms).

ideas.idea: TestParse_ValidDCP - Parse real Adobe DCP sample (Canon/Nikon), verify UniversalRecipe fields populated correctly (exposure, contrast near neutral)
TestReadTIFF_ValidFile - Verify TIFF structure parsed correctly, IFD accessible, magic bytes validated
TestReadTIFF_InvalidMagicBytes - Non-TIFF file should return error "invalid TIFF magic bytes"
TestExtractCameraProfileTag_Present - Tag 50740 exists and returns XML bytes
TestExtractCameraProfileTag_Missing - Tag 50740 missing should return error "not a valid DCP file"
TestParseProfile_ValidXML - Adobe Camera Profile XML unmarshals correctly, ProfileName extracted
TestParseProfile_MalformedXML - Invalid XML returns descriptive error
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_LinearCurve - Linear curve (0,0 → 255,255) returns zero exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_ExposureShift - Midpoint shift (+64) extracts positive exposure value
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_ContrastAdjustment - Steeper slope extracts positive contrast value
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_HighlightsShadows - Top-end and bottom-end curve adjustments extract highlights/shadows correctly
TestParse_MissingToneCurve - DCP without tone curve returns neutral recipe (all parameters zero)
TestParse_IdentityMatrix - Identity matrix detected, no warning logged
TestParse_NonIdentityMatrix - Non-identity matrix logs warning "color calibration not supported", stores matrix in metadata
TestParse_CorruptTIFF - Truncated TIFF file returns error without panic
TestParse_OutOfRangeValues - Tone curve points >255 or <0 are clamped correctly
Benchmark tests: BenchmarkParseDCP, BenchmarkAnalyzeToneCurve - verify <200ms performance target
